title: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD)
focus: Principles, benefits, and applications of QKD and Entanglement-Based QKD.
rationale: QKD is a foundational quantum communication concept frequently tested in UPSC for its security applications and mechanisms.
sectionExcerpt: QKD leverages quantum mechanics principles to ensure secure distribution of encryption keys. Entanglement-based QKD ensures secure key sharing even with compromised devices and detects eavesdropping because interception disturbs quantum states. Free-space QKD works without costly fibre optics.
evidenceBullets: QKD leverages quantum mechanics for secure encryption key distribution.
Entanglement-based QKD detects eavesdropping because interception disturbs quantum states.
Free-space QKD operates without fibre optics, making it suitable for tough terrains and dense cities.

title: National Quantum Mission (NQM)
focus: Objectives, implementing agency, and targets of the NQM, along with QpiAI's 25-qubit quantum computer.
rationale: Government missions are high-yield for UPSC prelims, especially regarding targets, nodal agencies, and specific thematic hubs.
sectionExcerpt: NQM aims to scale up R&D in Quantum Technology. It is implemented by the Department of Science and Technology and is one of the nine PMSTIAC initiatives. Objectives include a 2,000 km secure communication network, 1,000-qubit quantum computers, magnetometry, atomic clocks, and quantum materials design. It plans four T-Hubs. QpiAI launched India's first 25-qubit superconducting quantum computer (QpiAI-Indus).
evidenceBullets: NQM is implemented by the Department of Science and Technology (DST).
Aims to develop quantum computers with 1,000 qubits capacity and a 2,000 km communication network.
Plans to establish four thematic hubs (T-Hubs) in computing, communication, sensing, and materials.
QpiAI launched India's first 25-qubit superconducting quantum computer, QpiAI-Indus.

title: Quantum Information Scrambling
focus: Definition, mechanism, and significance of quantum information scrambling, and Google's Willow processor.
rationale: Conceptual understanding of quantum phenomena is a recurring theme in UPSC Science & Tech questions.
sectionExcerpt: Quantum information scrambling refers to the rapid spread of information across a quantum system via entanglement, making it inaccessible to local measurements but hiding it globally rather than erasing it. Google's quantum processor Willow achieved a ~13,000x speed-up over classical supercomputers, detecting the Quantum Echoes algorithm to prove information retention.
evidenceBullets: Quantum information scrambling distributes data among particles through entanglement, hiding it globally.
Information is not erased but becomes inaccessible to local measurements.
Google's Willow processor achieved a ~13,000x speed-up over top classical supercomputers.

title: QSIP (Quantum Random Number Generator System in Package)
focus: Features and applications of the QSIP quantum security chip.
rationale: A specific indigenous technological development dedicated to the nation, relevant for tech-based objective questions.
sectionExcerpt: QSIP is a quantum security chip dedicated to the nation by the Prime Minister. It provides quantum-certified randomness for cryptographic algorithms, ensuring defense against cyber threats and future quantum attacks.
evidenceBullets: QSIP is a quantum security chip providing quantum-certified randomness.
It is used in cryptographic algorithms to defend against cyber threats and future quantum attacks.

title: Brain-Computer Interface (BCI)
focus: Mechanism, applications, and recent developments in BCI technology.
rationale: BCI is an emerging technology with significant medical and cognitive applications, making it highly relevant for UPSC S&T.
sectionExcerpt: A Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) enables direct brain-to-device communication by translating neural signals into commands, bypassing muscular control. Stanford scientists developed a privacy-focused BCI requiring a mental password. Applications include restoring mobility/speech, mental wellness, gaming, and cognitive enhancement.
evidenceBullets: BCI translates neural signals into commands, allowing device operation via thought alone, bypassing muscular control.
Can use invasive implants or non-invasive wearables to acquire brain activity.
Applications include restoring mobility for paralysis/stroke patients and cognitive enhancement.
Stanford developed a privacy-focused BCI requiring a mental password before decoding thoughts.
